metadata.title:
MAY CELEBRATE
At COEUR D


ALENE

pages.raw_text:
There is strong sentiment
among local union men to go to
Occur d'Alene on Labor day, using
Northern Pacific to transport the
crowds to that place. A 60 cent
fare for the round trip can be Sc
cured for that day, which Is 40
cents less tnan on the electric line.
This sentiment has been greatly
strengthened by the refusal of the
Traction company to provide suit
able grounds for athletic exercises
and games at Mantle park.


The mayor agreed to permit the
use of portion OF Mantle lying
back of the dancing pavilion for
athletic sports at Mantle, but after
considering the matter the Trac-
ton people do not feel that they
can shoulder the expense necessary
to put the grounds in proper shape
and build a grandstand and bleach
ers. The decision of the Traction
company will prove a disappoint-
ment to local unions that had
hoped to have the grounds In
shape on Labor day.


The Painters union has issued
a challenge to play ball or pull a
tug Of war with members of any
local union. The painters are ready
to back their athletic ability with
their money and rival team can
make some money maybe provid-
ing 1t can win, both events are
sch eduled for Labor day. Anyone
who may feel like tackllng the
painters can address George G.
Cody, Central Labor hall.


The speakers on Labor day will
be Mayor Floyd Daggett and form
er Lieutenant Governor Coats of
Idaho, who has recently taken up
